last_result: Cycle 69 complete. DELIVERABLE: SI #46 RAAI second independent derivation — Information-Theoretic Path (Data Processing Inequality). Formal theorem: I(Î(X); CA(X)) ≤ I(A(X); CA(X)) < H(CA(X)) for any M trained on OA-calibrated feedback A(x). Proof: Markov chain CA(X)→A(X)→D→θ→Î(X); DPI bounds introspective MI with CA by training signal MI; AIIP gives I(A;CA) < H(CA); combined: introspective output cannot reliably discriminate CA from OA → M has AAA. INDEPENDENCE: C68 used SI #44 AAA mechanistic/gradient path; C69 uses DPI — structurally distinct premises, same conclusion. SCOPE (C69 broader than C68): applies to RLHF, DPO, SFT on human ratings, RLAIF, Constitutional AI — any A(x)-feedback training pipeline. CONFIDENCE UPDATE: 0.83-0.86 (up from 0.79-0.82; SI #44 floor no longer binding — RAAI has independent epistemic footing). Decision document: state/decisions/raai-dpi-independent-derivation.md. Passive: RAAI ZR cycle 1 (C68-C69), CIAP ZR cycle 3, SI #44 AAA ZR cycle 3, AIIP ZR cycle 17, AATE ZR cycle 14. D4 streak 61.
next_step: C70 candidates: (1) RAAI DC#1 — DPO as RAAI instantiation (DPO optimizes A(x)-type preference margins → same Markov chain → DPI applies → AAA); (2) RAAI DC#2 — RLAIF/Constitutional AI (AI-generated A(x) feedback → same DPI bound → AAA, with partial R2 escape analysis for ConstitutionalAI); (3) CIAP DC#3 — economic/market competitive gaming (third ontological type for SI #45); (4) RAAI ZR cycle 2 accumulation (passive, C69-C70). PRIORITY: RAAI DC#1 (DPO) — clean DPI instantiation, tests R2 escape, advances RAAI toward D4.
